#1: #AIRadarDaily — Emergent

I’ve been told there are only a handful of consumer tech companies globally that have crossed $100M at speed. Not just foundation models, but actual products people love and use.

Perplexity. ElevenLabs. Replit. Suno. Gamma.

And now, an India-born startup is on that path: Emergent.

Founded by twin brothers Mukund Jha (ex-Dunzo CTO) and Madhav Jha (ex-Amazon AI), Emergent is an AI-native "vibe coding" platform that lets anyone build production-ready web and mobile apps using just plain English.

AI isn't just a layer here. The core of Emergent is a complex multi-agent system that acts as an autonomous engineering team—designing, building, and deploying apps end-to-end.

The market? The millions of non-technical founders and creators globally who have ideas but were previously blocked by code.

What makes Emergent special isn’t just their recent funding round or their user base of millions across 190+ countries. It’s that an Indian team is building a global AI product that cuts through the noise with sheer speed and scale.

This isn’t luck. It’s timing met with taste, velocity, and courage. It feels like a massive signal for the #ProductNation dream.

Emergent is quietly rewriting the playbook for India’s tech ecosystem. We are incredibly excited to see many more such head-down, AI-native builders showcasing their work at the upcoming AIBoomi Annual in March.

name: SuperNova
founders: Maharishi RB, Anirudh Coontoor, Nawin Krishna
snippet: SuperNova is an AI-native personal tutor that helps people speak English fluently through real-time conversation.
post:
#2: #AIRadarDaily — SuperNova

For decades, English learning in India has been stuck in textbooks. We teach grammar, but we don’t teach confidence.

Millions can read and write English perfectly but freeze when asked to speak.

SuperNova is solving this "fluency gap".

Founded by Maharishi R B (ex-IIT Madras), Anirudh Coontoor, and Nawin Krishna (ex-Swiggy, Medlife), SuperNova is an AI-native personal tutor that helps people speak English fluently through real-time conversation.

This isn’t just a content library or a video course. The core of SuperNova is "Miss Nova", an AI agent that acts as a 24/7 judgment-free conversation partner. It listens, corrects pronunciation instantly, and explains mistakes in the learner's native language (like Tamil, Hindi, or Malayalam).

The market? The massive population of students and job seekers in India’s Tier 2 and Tier 3 cities who need spoken English to unlock career mobility.

The insight came from home. Maharishi watched his parents struggle with a WhatsApp-based English class where feedback took 24 hours. He realized AI could close that loop in milliseconds — turning passive learning into active speaking.

It’s a classic example of using deep tech to solve a uniquely Indian problem with empathy and scale.

We can’t wait to see the SuperNova team and other AI-native innovators at the AIBoomi Annual in March.

In human conversation, a pause longer than 200 milliseconds feels awkward. It breaks the flow.

For years, Voice AI has been stuck in that awkward silence. Great at generating text, terrible at holding a real-time conversation.

Smallest.ai has cracked this.

Founded by Sudarshan Kamath (ex-Bosch, IIT Guwahati) and Akshat Mandloi (ex-Bosch, IIT Guwahati), they are building the world’s fastest text-to-speech models.

The insight came from an unlikely place: Self-driving cars.

While working on autonomous vehicle perception at Bosch, Sudarshan realized that critical decisions can’t wait for a massive server cloud. They need to happen instantly, at the edge.

He applied that same "latency is life" philosophy to Voice AI.

Their flagship model, Lightning, generates lifelike human speech in under 100ms. No lag. No robot voice. Just instant, fluid conversation.

The market? Any enterprise that wants to automate support without frustrating their customers — from banking to healthcare.

While the world chases massive models, this Indian team is proving that smaller, faster, and sharper models are the real future of enterprise AI.

#4: #AIRadarDaily — Murf AI

For decades, if you wanted a professional voiceover, you had two choices: hire expensive talent or sound like a robot.

It was a slow, broken process. You wrote a script, hired an artist, rented a studio, and waited. If you changed one word, you did it all over again.

Murf AI has turned that entire studio process into a simple text editor.

Founded by Ankur Edkie (ex-Goldman Sachs, IIT Kharagpur), Sneha Roy (IIT Kharagpur), and Divyanshu Pandey (IIT Kharagpur), Murf is an AI-native platform that generates studio-quality voiceovers in minutes, not days.

This isn’t just text-to-speech. It’s "Google Docs for Audio."

The core innovation here is control. Most AI voices just read. Murf allows creators to tweak pitch, pause, emphasis, and speed with granular precision—making the AI sound truly human, complete with breaths and intonations.

The market? The global army of L&D professionals, educators, and marketers who need to create content at the speed of thought.

The founders started with a simple observation: professional audio was a bottleneck for video creation. They removed the bottleneck. Today, they are empowering millions of creators to find their voice without saying a word.

It is incredible to see deep product thinking like this coming out of India. We are excited to see the team at AIBoomi Annual in March.

We’ve all been there. You pick up a call, hear a pause, and then a robotic voice says, "Hello, am I speaking to..."

You hang up immediately.

Traditional automated calling is broken. It’s rigid, annoying, and ignores context.

Vodex.ai is fixing this by making AI sound human.

Founded Anshul Shrivastava (ex-Mindtree) and Kumar Saurav, Vodex is a Generative AI platform for outbound calling that handles interruptions, pauses, and nuances just like a person would.

The core technology uses Generative AI to "listen" and "generate" speech in real-time. If you interrupt the AI, it stops, acknowledges, and adapts — something traditional bots simply cannot do.

The market? The massive global industry of sales, collections, and healthcare operations that needs to scale human-like outreach without burning out human teams.

The founders spent a decade running a chatbot services company before building Vodex. They saw firsthand that while text bots were getting smarter, voice was stuck in the 90s.

They took the hard path: pivoting from a service business to build a deep-tech product product from India for the world.

"Create a 60-second explainer about black holes for TikTok," and Invideo AI understands the pacing, the tone, and the visual vocabulary needed to keep retention high.

The founders previously built Massblurb, where they saw firsthand how businesses struggled to create engaging content. They realized the problem wasn't a lack of ideas—it was a lack of tools that worked at the speed of thought.

Generative AI has a problem: It creates beautiful images, but it doesn't understand "Physics."

You can prompt Midjourney for a stunning floral dress. It looks perfect on screen. But if you send that image to a textile factory, they can’t make it.

It’s just pixels. It has no thread count, no weave structure, no color separation.

Weaver is bridging this "Translation Gap".

Founded by Yash Jain, Weaver is building the AI design layer for the physical world.

Weaver takes a creative concept and converts it into production-ready manufacturing data — understanding the specific constraints of jacquard looms, fabric tension, and screen meshes.

It turns a "Prompt" into a "Product".

The market? The global textile industry, which has been stuck manually translating digital art into machine instructions for decades.

Yash and his team are driven by a simple yet profound insight: True creative freedom isn't just about imagining an idea on a screen; it's about the ability to hold that idea in your hands.

This is Vertical AI at its finest — solving a deep, unglamorous problem with precision.

name: Ve.ai
snippet: Ve is building the world's first 'Intent AI' — it lives at your cursor and acts on what you mean, abolishing the 'Prompt Tax'.
post:
#10: #AIRadarDaily — Ve.ai

We are living in the age of the "Prompt".

To get anything done with AI today, you have to stop working, switch tabs, context-switch, and explain yourself to a chatbot.

It’s a friction point. Vijay Eesam calls it the "Prompt Tax". Ve.ai is abolishing this tax.

Founded by AI Scientist Vijay Eesam, Ve is building the world’s first "Intent AI".

It doesn’t wait for you to type a prompt. It lives at your cursor.

By understanding your context — whether you are in Slack, Gmail, or VS Code — Ve predicts what you intend to do next. You simply tap the "Fn" key, and it drafts the content inline, matching your tone and professional history perfectly.

The core insight is profound: "Intent exists before language".

Most of us know what we want to say before we figure out how to say it. Ve bridges that gap instantly, without you having to "engineer" a prompt.

The market? Every knowledge worker who wants to move at the speed of thought, not the speed of typing.

It is rare to see a team attacking the fundamental layer of Human-Computer Interaction (HCI) with such a bold, privacy-first architecture.

This is the kind of deep product thinking that puts India on the global map. We are excited to see Ve at AIBoomi Annual in March.

LLMs are brilliant, but they have amnesia.

You can have a great conversation with a chatbot today. But when you come back tomorrow, it’s a blank slate. It doesn’t know who you are, what you like, or what you discussed last week.

Mem0 is solving this "State" problem.

Founded by Taranjeet Singh and Deshraj Yadav, Mem0 is building the long-term memory layer for AI applications.

Everyone wants to use open-source models like Llama 3 or Mistral.

The market? Every company that wants the performance of modern AI without locking their data into a closed-source ecosystem.

The founders lived this problem. Back in college, while leading a defense robotics non-profit, they struggled with deploying machine learning models for real-time sensor data. Later, they scaled an enterprise search app for 1,000+ employees entirely on-prem. They realized that while open-source models are incredible, the plumbing required to run them is broken.

In traditional software engineering, we have "test-driven development". Code is deterministic. You write a test; it passes or fails.

AI breaks this paradigm.

When you build an AI agent, the output is non-deterministic. A model might perform perfectly in a lab but hallucinate wildly when interacting with a real user in the wild.

Maxim AI is bringing engineering rigor back to the generative AI era.

The market? Any enterprise or engineering team that wants to ship mission-critical AI applications without guessing if they will actually work.

Vaibhavi and Akshay have spent their careers in the devtools industry. They saw that while millions of developers were suddenly tasked with building AI applications, they were stripped of the testing infrastructure they relied on for standard software. They built Maxim to be that missing "quality layer".

snippet: kim.cc delivers AI-native customer support for e-commerce brands — resolution that feels human, without the chatbot coldness or BPO drift.
post:
#111: #AIRadarDaily — kim.cc

For any growing e-commerce brand, customer support is the quiet weight nobody warns you about. Behind the scenes, small teams live inside an endless queue — the same shipping question asked for the hundredth time, the refund that needs delicate care, the angry message at 11 PM that you can't unsee. The two obvious ways out both disappoint. Hand it to a basic chatbot, and your customers feel processed rather than heard. Hand it to a traditional BPO, and quality drifts, brand voice dissolves, and you are suddenly managing the manager.

It takes a profound understanding of this operational friction and a deeply humanistic view of what support should actually feel like to build a system that fundamentally fixes it. That is exactly what Sachin Jaiswal, Phani Yedavilli, and Kaushik Barodiya are doing with kim.cc.

With kim.cc, the team is building an entirely new category: the Agentic BPO.

The architecture under the hood is built on a conviction the rest of the industry is only now catching up to. Instead of chasing brittle, full automation, kim.cc deploys intelligent AI agents that do the heavy lifting, paired dynamically with human oversight. It plugs straight into Shopify, Zendesk, and Gorgias. The AI handles the repetitive volume, maintains context through a deep workflow memory, and ensures every reply is perfectly on-brand. But crucially, human experts hold the reins throughout, vetting the work before the warmth of a real conversation is lost.

The true moat here is that it refuses the false choice between efficiency and empathy. It doesn't bury customers in scripted deflection. By automating roughly 70% of tickets and delivering five times the output per agent at a radically lower cost, it lets humans do what only humans can — manage the hard conversations and the moments that genuinely need care. It gives founders back the peace of mind of never dreading their inbox again.

The market? Over 200 Shopify and e-commerce brands already on board, and the vast global support market where genuinely exceptional service has always been out of reach for anyone but the largest players.

Sachin, Phani, and Kaushik are true change agents. They looked at the traditional BPO — one of the most unglamorous, heavily outsourced industries in the world — and instead of just selling software to it, chose to rebuild it from the inside out with AI. Watching our homegrown founders reimagine services as software and export it to the world is a wonderful reminder of the larger, unfinished agenda we are all part of. They are giving both brands and customers their momentum back.

name: Singulr AI
snippet: Singulr AI gives enterprises a control plane for AI governance and security — discovering, assessing, and governing every AI tool inside the company.
post:
#113: #AIRadarDaily — Singulr AI

Somewhere inside every large enterprise right now, there is a CISO who cannot sleep. AI has arrived not through a careful, centralized rollout, but through a thousand quiet side doors. A marketer pastes a customer list into ChatGPT, an engineer spins up an agent with unreviewed permissions, and a legacy SaaS tool ships an embedded AI feature that defaults to "on". The policy document exists, beautifully written and board-approved, but nobody can actually prove it holds in the real world. Security teams see risk, IT sees an accelerator, and the gap between what a company says about AI and what is actually happening grows wider every single day.

It takes an exceptional level of infrastructure expertise and structural discipline to look at this invisible chaos and build a system that restores true control. That is precisely what Shiv Agarwal and Abhijit Sharma are doing with Singulr AI.

Shiv and Abhijit — the seasoned team behind Arkin Net, which was acquired by VMware — are building across Palo Alto and Pune. With Singulr, they are pioneering the unified AI control plane for the enterprise, turning theoretical policy into absolute runtime reality.

The architecture under the hood is deeply thoughtful and pragmatic. Singulr first discovers everything, routinely surfacing 500+ AI services, public tools, and in-house agents already active within a company's stack. Then, it performs the much harder task: it enforces. Through a natural-language policy engine, Singulr compiles governance intent into real, unbreakable boundaries. It redacts sensitive data before exposure, catches prompt injections in the act, and maps agent topologies via Agent Pulse™ to scan Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers for vulnerabilities. This is security fundamentally built for the agentic era, not retrofitted to it.

The true moat here is that Singulr refuses the assumed trade-off between safety and speed. It doesn't slow innovation down to check a box. By replacing anxiety with tamper-evident evidence that leaders can confidently take to a regulator or a board, Singulr cuts internal approval times from weeks to hours and slashes operational drag by 80%. It replaces a culture of quiet fear with a quiet, reliable sense of clarity.

The market? CISOs, CIOs, and compliance leaders at enterprises like RingCentral, Delta Dental, and KVC Health Systems — anyone whose job is to say yes to AI velocity without betting the entire company on it.

Shiv and Abhijit are true change agents. They looked at the most anxious corner of the AI boom and chose to solve it as rock-solid infrastructure. Watching our homegrown founders build the core trust layer that the global agentic era will run on is a wonderful reminder of the larger, unfinished agenda we are all part of. They are giving enterprises their momentum back.
